title: Inclusive Interaction, Content, Responsive Design, and Human Evaluation · Evidence Triage
summary: A preserved decision record for inclusive interaction, content, responsive design, and human evaluation contains one plausible evidence, scope, design, implementation, evaluation, or ownership defect. Find the first unsupported claim before changing the product. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in accessible responsive product experience and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice,…
objectives: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.
Verify complete keyboard operation, logical order, visible focus, focus movement, escape paths, and no traps across state changes.
Design content and interaction to reflow at zoom and changed mobile, tablet, desktop, orientation, text, and language conditions.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-1 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-1
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Inspect the failure record · Debug Semantic Task Flow
steps.instruction:
debug case — The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.

Inspect the Semantic Task Flow trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Inspect the failure record · Debug Semantic Task Flow.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order. For Inspect the failure record · Debug Semantic Task Flow, keep Semantic Task Flow connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Semantic Task Flow, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

hints: For Inspect the failure record · Debug Semantic Task Flow, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Semantic Task Flow precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Inspect the failure record · Debug Semantic Task Flow, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.

For Inspect the failure record · Debug Semantic Task Flow, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: ARIA can repair any non-semantic interaction after implementation.

stimulus.title: Semantic Task Flow case record
stimulus.caption: Three excerpts from a learner evidence packet; only one connects a claim to a reproducible check.

lines.label: assumption
lines.text: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The debug variation 4 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order.

lines.label: risk
lines.text: ARIA can repair any non-semantic interaction after implementation.

lines.label: evidence
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Structure landmarks, headings, names, instructions, controls, errors, and status around the user task and reading order. Artifact under review: accessible responsive product experience.

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
The record names the assumption, applies Semantic Task Flow, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.
The record says the result looks right but supplies no quantity, trace, condition, or counterexample. That claim does not survive the inspect the failure record evidence boundary.

steps.title: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Human Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
Append a pp1-human-accessibility-eval-151aza: manifest entry with concrete verify plus failure, owner, or repair evidence.

Evidence target: Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Human Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Human Accessibility Eval into observable performance evidence. For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Human Accessibility Eval, keep Human Accessibility Eval connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.code:
pp1-human-accessibility-eval-151aza:
  verify: [replace with a deterministic check]
  failure: [replace with the rejected condition]
  repair: [replace with a local reproduction and correction path]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.

hints: For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Human Accessibility Eval, name the types, ownership, and stop path, Human Accessibility Eval precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Human Accessibility Eval, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The debug variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

For Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Human Accessibility Eval, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One accessibility tool can certify WCAG conformance.

Order the Human Accessibility Eval evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

steps.title: Publish the incident record · Debug Human Accessibility Eval
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Human Accessibility Eval: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — The primary workflow works by mouse at one desktop width but loses reading order, focus, status, error recovery, and meaning under changed access needs. The debug variation 2 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.

Evidence target: Publish the incident record · Debug Human Accessibility Eval.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Publish the incident record · Debug Human Accessibility Eval, keep Human Accessibility Eval connected to mechanically reviewable stakeholder, affected-user, artifact, changed-case, failure, repair, release, recovery, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Combine automated checks, keyboard review, screen-reader or structured-text inspection, zoom, contrast, and affected-user evidence with documented limits.
